english: thing is brought about quite of itself Mil.; so esp. in the phrase: lhún-gyis grúb-pa (lhun gyis grub pa) being produced spontaneously (opp. to making, procuring) e. g. clothes, food etc. were always at his disposal, viz. in a supernatural way Dzl.; dpál-las grub (dpal las grub) it devolved on me in consequence of my perfection, my superior qualities Mil.; dón-la grúb-pa (don la grub pa) med kyan (med kyang) though it did not actually happen so (still, being meant to frighten by appearances etc.) Glr.; byéd-na don čen .grub (byed na don chen 'grub) if you do so, you will have many advantages (lit. great welfare) by it Mil.; gru .grúb-pa (gru 'grub pa) Tar. 25,6; 34,20 Schf.: to take in a full cargo, though from the wide meaning of the word, it may also signify: to accomplish a journey happily, so esp. in the passage Tar. 35,3 — 2. to be made, fabricated, rdó-las (rdo las) out of stone. — 3. to be fulfilled, granted, of wishes etc., also with bźin-du (bzhin du). — 4. to be performed according to rule, of charms; cf. sgrúb-pa (sgrub pa) and .grúb-pa ('grub pa). — .grúb-sbyór ('grub sbyor) is an expression occurring in almanacs, relative to the proving true of certain astrological prognostics of good luck, similar to, but not identic with rten-.brél (rten 'brel).
pf. grum (grum) (?), to pinch or nip off (the point of a thing), to cut off, to prune, lop, clip, the wings, W., cf. .grúm-pa ('grum pa).

I. 1. to walk, to pass, to travel, .grúl-bar byéd-pa ('grul bar byed pa) to cause to go, to send off, despatch, a messenger Dzl.; *ńùn-la ḍul* (*sngun la 'grul*) W. walk first! take the lead! .grúl-ba-po ('grul ba po), .grúl-po ('grul po) Sch., *ḍúl-kan* (*'grul mkhan*), *ḍúl-mi* (*'grul mi*) W. a walker, foot-traveller, pedestrian; .grúl-pa ('grul pa) Sch. id.; .grúl ('grul) sbst. passage, the possibility of passing, nya-ńàń-gi .grul čád-pas (nya ngan gi 'grul chad pas) the passing from Nyanan being made impracticable (viz. by snow) Mil. — 2. fig. to walk, to live, act, or behave, *ṭím-si* (*khrims si*) (or *ṭím-mi* (*khrims mi*)) nań-tar* (*nang ltar*) W. (to live) in conformity with one's duty, in accordance to the law. — 3. to pass, to be good, current, of coins. II. i. o. brúl-ba (brul ba) Mil.
1. pf. of .grú-ba ('gru ba). — 2. sbst. zeal, diligence, endeavour; more frq. brtson-.grús (brtson 'grus).

1. to roll one's self, sá-la (sa la) on the ground; .gre-ldóg ('gre ldog) Glr. (or .gre lóg ('gre log) Pth.) byéd-pa (byed pa) id., e.g. from pain, despair etc.; also of horses etc. — 2. to repeat Cs.

(cf. sgreń-ba (sgreng ba)) to stand (not in use in W.) dón-gi kar .gréń-nas (don gi khar 'greng nas) standing at the top of the pit Dzl.; dńáńs-pa ltar .gréń-bar gyur (dngangs pa ltar 'greng bar gyur) they start up as if frightened Dzl.; of the po-mtsán (pho mtshan): to be erected Med.; mi .greń gsum (mi 'greng gsum) three lengths of a man Tar. — .gréń-bu ('greng bu), also .gréń-po ('greng po) (Glr.) the sign of the vowel e.

pf. bkram (bkram), fut. dgram (dgram), imp. kroms (khroms) (W. *ṭam-će* (*bkram ce*), imp. *ṭom* (*khrom*)) 1. to put or lay down in order, e.g. beams, spars etc. B. and col ; to spread out, to display, goods, books, on the table or ground; to scatter, blossoms by the wind Stg.; to draw, a curtain. — 2. to sprinkle, water, B. and col. — 3. to distribute, for .brim-pa ('brim pa) C.

pf. bkral (bkral), another form for grol-ba (grol ba), to explain, comment, illustrate, dgóńs-pa (dgongs pa) the import (of the words or writings of the saints); .grél-ba ('grel ba) Cs., .grel-pa ('grel pa) Zam., Tar. explanation, explication, commentary; don-.grél (don 'grel), resp. dgońs-.grél (dgongs 'grel) explanation of the meaning; tsig-.grél (tshig 'grel) explanation of a word, of the words; gźuń-.grél (gzhung 'grel) 1. explication of the text 2. text and commentary; rań-.grél (rang 'grel) prob. self-explanation, an explanation contained in the book itself Wdn.; .grél-ba-po ('grel ba po) Cs., .grél-po ('grel po) Sch., .grel-byéd ('grel byed) Cs. an explainer, commentator. (pan-tsún .grél-ba (pan tsun 'grel ba), and kral .grél-ba (khral 'grel ba) Lexx.?)
C. expense, expenditure, of money, *ḍo-gor táń-ba* (*'gro sgor gtang ba*) to lay out (money), to spend.
Dramila, country in the south of India Schf.; another reading gives Draviḍa (coast of Coromandel).
I. vb., pf. and imp. soń (song), the imp. .gro(s) ('gro(s)) seldom used, 1. to
